title: K-means over k=2..5
summary: Basic k-means sweep across a range of cluster counts.
oce_text:
[["clustering::segment"]]
input = "./data/respondents.csv"
uuid_col = "respondent_id"
vars = ["q1", "q2", "q3", "q4"]
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "kmeans_demo"
na_action = "complete_cases"
random_seed = 42
headers = true
methods = [
  { method = "kmeans", k_range = [2, 5] },
]

title: Kmeans + hier + kmedoids at k=3
summary: Compare three partition methods at a single k.
oce_text:
[["clustering::segment"]]
input = "./data/respondents.csv"
uuid_col = "respondent_id"
vars = ["q1", "q2", "q3", "q4"]
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "multi_demo"
random_seed = 42
headers = true
methods = [
  { method = "kmeans", k_range = [3, 3] },
  { method = "hier",   k_range = [3, 3], hier_method = "ward.D2" },
  { method = "kmedoids", k_range = [3, 3] },
]

title: Consensus ensemble over three sources
summary: Fit three base clusterers and combine via co-association matrix + PAM.
oce_text:
[["clustering::segment"]]
input = "./data/respondents.csv"
uuid_col = "respondent_id"
vars = ["q1", "q2", "q3", "q4"]
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "ensemble_demo"
random_seed = 42
headers = true
methods = [
  { method = "kmeans",   k_range = [3, 3] },
  { method = "hier",     k_range = [3, 3], hier_method = "ward.D2" },
  { method = "kmedoids", k_range = [3, 3] },
  { method = "ensemble", k_range = [3, 3], engine = "pam", depends_on = ["ensemble_demo_kmeans_k3", "ensemble_demo_hier_k3", "ensemble_demo_kmedoids_k3"] },
]

title: Archetypal analysis
summary: Best-of-nrep Cutler-Breiman archetypes with projected-gradient inner solver.
oce_text:
[["clustering::segment"]]
input = "./data/respondents.csv"
uuid_col = "respondent_id"
vars = ["q1", "q2", "q3", "q4"]
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "archetypes_demo"
random_seed = 42
headers = true
methods = [
  { method = "archetypes", k_range = [3, 4], nrep = 20, maxit = 100, inner_iters = 50 },
]

title: Affinity propagation targeting K
summary: AP with preference bisection to land on a specified cluster count.
oce_text:
[["clustering::segment"]]
input = "./data/respondents.csv"
uuid_col = "respondent_id"
vars = ["q1", "q2", "q3", "q4"]
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "affinity_demo"
random_seed = 42
headers = true
methods = [
  { method = "affinity", k_range = [3, 3], r = 2.0, prc = 10.0, q = 0.9 },
]

title: Latent class (categorical manifest)
summary: EM latent-class model on integer-valued survey items.
oce_text:
[["clustering::segment"]]
input = "./data/survey_items.csv"
uuid_col = "respondent_id"
vars = ["item_q1", "item_q2", "item_q3", "item_q4", "item_q5"]
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "lca_demo"
na_action = "complete_cases"
random_seed = 2026
headers = true
methods = [
  { method = "latent_class", k_range = [2, 5], nrep = 20, maxiter = 5000, tol = 1e-10 },
]

title: Unsupervised LDA (tied-covariance GMM)
summary: Shared-covariance Gaussian mixture fit by EM — the unsupervised analogue of LDA. Posterior class gate is a softmax of linear discriminant scores.
oce_text:
[["clustering::segment"]]
input = "./data/respondents.csv"
uuid_col = "respondent_id"
vars = ["pref_quality", "pref_price", "pref_brand", "pref_speed"]
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "ulda_demo"
random_seed = 42
headers = true
methods = [
  { method = "gmm", k_range = [2, 5], covariance = "tied", nrep = 5, em_iter = 200 },
]

title: Gaussian mixture model
summary: EM-fit Gaussian mixture with k-means warm-start; diagonal, full, or tied covariances.
oce_text:
[["clustering::segment"]]
input = "./data/respondents.csv"
uuid_col = "respondent_id"
vars = ["pref_quality", "pref_price", "pref_brand", "pref_speed"]
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "gmm_demo"
random_seed = 42
headers = true
methods = [
  { method = "gmm", k_range = [2, 5], covariance = "full", nrep = 5, em_iter = 200 },
]

title: HDBSCAN density-based
summary: Density-based clustering across a range of min_pts values; noise rows go to cluster 0.
oce_text:
[["clustering::segment"]]
input = "./data/respondents.csv"
uuid_col = "respondent_id"
vars = ["q1", "q2", "q3", "q4"]
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "hdbscan_demo"
headers = true
methods = [
  { method = "hdbscan", min_pts_range = [5, 10], noise_policy = "own_cluster" },
]
